text.title: Build In Public Cadence Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the Build In Public Cadence methodology. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Each weekday runs ALL four steps: capture → write a hook → ship one post → log result. Skipping any step breaks the rhythm.
rule.rationale: Each weekday runs ALL four steps: capture → write a hook → ship one post → log result. Skipping any step breaks the rhythm.

rule.statement: Weekly post on long-form channel (IH / newsletter) is the SOURCE; X / LinkedIn posts atomize from it. Multi-source writing doubles cost.
rule.rationale: Weekly post on long-form channel (IH / newsletter) is the SOURCE; X / LinkedIn posts atomize from it. Multi-source writing doubles cost.

rule.statement: Daily session is capped at 30 minutes. Going over = scope creep; abort and reschedule.
rule.rationale: Daily session is capped at 30 minutes. Going over = scope creep; abort and reschedule.

rule.statement: Spec names the single founder. 'team' / 'we' / '' forbidden.
rule.rationale: Spec names the single founder. 'team' / 'we' / '' forbidden.

rule.statement: Long-form post happens the SAME weekday every week (default Tuesday). Drifting day = no cadence.
rule.rationale: Long-form post happens the SAME weekday every week (default Tuesday). Drifting day = no cadence.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent's context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-build-in-public-cadence.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
